title: Junta Jets Strike Yaw Region — Six Fighter Aircraft Drop Eight Bombs
detail: On the afternoon of March 24, the SAC launched an unprovoked airstrike against the Maulay junction area on the border of Gangaw and Htilin townships in Yaw region (Magway Region). At 1:22 PM, four fighter jets dropped six 500-pound bombs and fired three rockets; a follow-on strike at 2:43 PM saw two more jets drop two 300-pound bombs. Five men were confirmed injured in the attack. Local sources reported no active resistance presence in the immediate strike area at the time of the attack.

title: Analysis: Russian Weapons and 'Meat Assault' Tactics From Ukraine Now Shaping Myanmar War
detail: Al Jazeera published an in-depth analysis on March 24, 2026, documenting how Russian military equipment and battlefield doctrine drawn from the Ukraine war are materially shaping the Myanmar civil war. Russia has emerged as the junta's most important defense partner, supplying advanced drones including the fixed-wing Albatross-M5 UAV, the Orlan-10E (capable of 16 hours airborne with optical/thermal imaging), and the kamikaze VT-40. The junta has also adopted Russia's 'meat assault' strategy — mass infantry waves against fortified positions — deploying conscripts in frontal assaults to deplete resistance ammunition. ACLED recorded 5,912 airstrikes and 931 drone attacks between February 2021 and March 13, 2026, with at least 4,865 and 366 deaths respectively from those categories. Air attack deaths rose 52% in 2025 compared to 2024, per UN figures.

title: AA Launches Concurrent Assaults on Ann and Ngape — Coordinated Multi-Front Rakhine Offensive
detail: On March 24, concurrent with the ongoing assault on the Shwe Min Gan Naval Base near Sittwe (launched March 23), the Arakan Army launched additional offensive operations against remaining SAC positions in Ann Township and Ngape Township in Magway Region (on the border of Rakhine). Ann Township — previously the headquarters of the Western Regional Military Command before its capture in December 2024 — still hosts some residual SAC infrastructure and the coordinated attack on multiple points demonstrates the AA's strategy of denying the junta any foothold across the Rakhine-Magway corridor. The multi-axis offensive on March 23–24 represents the most significant AA push since the Ann capture in December.

title: PDF Landmine Ambush Kills 2 Junta Soldiers in Minhla Township
detail: On March 24, a PDF unit (Thayarwady District Battalion 3 / Strategy 22 PDF) ambushed a retreating junta column of approximately 50 troops near U To Bama Su village in Minhla Township, Bago Region, using a landmine. Two SAC soldiers were confirmed killed and three injured. The Bago Region continues to be an active front as PDF forces harry SAC troop movements along the Irrawaddy River corridor. The ambush reflects the continuing effectiveness of PDF hit-and-run tactics against junta formations.
